entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Grain crop produced by smallholders in Tanzania

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: Country where the study on sorghum seed systems was conducted

entities.name: FORMAL SEED SYSTEM
entities.description: A seed delivery system characterized by official channels and regulated access

entities.name: INFORMAL SEED SYSTEM
entities.description: A seed delivery system based on farmer-to-farmer exchange and traditional methods

entities.name: INTEGRATED SEED SYSTEM
entities.description: A seed system combining both formal and informal channels

entities.name: MULTINOMIAL LOGIT MODEL
entities.description: Statistical model used to analyze the drivers of seed system choice

entities.name: SURVEY DATA
entities.description: Data collected from 1492 households to analyze seed system choices

entities.name: FINANCIAL LITERACY
entities.description: Knowledge of financial management associated with formal seed system use

entities.name: CROP INSURANCE
entities.description: Insurance products for crops associated with formal seed system use

entities.name: EXTENSION SERVICES
entities.description: Agricultural advisory services associated with formal seed system use

entities.name: FINANCIAL SERVICES
entities.description: Access to credit and financial tools associated with formal seed system use

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: Agricultural practice of growing two or more crops together, associated with integrated seed systems

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: Ability of crop varieties to withstand environmental stresses

entities.name: IMPROVED VARIETIES
entities.description: Seed varieties developed through crop improvement programs for better performance

entities.name: PEERS
entities.description: Other farmers who provide information on seed, agronomy, and markets

entities.name: MARKET ACCESS
entities.description: Proximity and participation in input and output markets
